subject: Support for addition of INDEX token to tosca functions
commit_message:
Support for addition of INDEX token to tosca functions

Providing the capability to add the index token to th tosca function of all types

Issue-ID: SDC-4472
Signed-off-by: Imam hussain <imam.hussain@est.tech>
Change-Id: Ib7ac80f31710101f50de76bdb7c79abdc637cfe3

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-2

I believe the intention of the story is to introduce support for the INDEX keyword rather than an integer index. Nevertheless, support for an integer index is also supported by the spec so there is value in supporting it.

However to fulfil the purpose of the story we need to add support for the INDEX keyword. This can be done as part of a separate story/review if you like, just make sure to update the description of the existing story accordingly. See chapter 2.20.2 for description of use of INDEX keyword in https://docs.oasis-open.org/tosca/TOSCA-Simple-Profile-YAML/v1.3/os/TOSCA-Simple-Profile-YAML-v1.3-os.html#_Toc26969440.

On whats implemented:
1. The index is being created as a string value when should be an integer, e.g. 
        heightInUnits:
          get_property:
          - floorPlan_0
          - depthInUnits
          - '1'
should be:
        heightInUnits:
          get_property:
          - floorPlan_0
          - depthInUnits
          - 1

2. I believe use of index only makes sense when the property/input etc is of type list
3. The index can be used at any point in the tree of subproperties where the type is list, for example, given:

data_types:
  my_complex_type:
    properties:
      list_complex_prop:
        type: list
        schema:
          my_nested_complex_type

  my_nested_compelx_type:
    properties:
      nested_list_complex_prop:
        type: list
        schema: string
node_types:
my_node_type:
  properties:
    my_prop1: 
      type: string
    my_prop2:
      type: my_complex_type

then a value can be set on my_prop1 as:
my_prop1: { get_property: [ my_prop2, list_complex_prop, 1, nested_list_complex_prop, 3]

meaning take the element at index 1 of the list in list_complex_prop and then get the element at index 3 in the list nested_list_complex_prop, i.e. given the following value for my_prop2:

my_prop2:
  list_complex_prop:
    - nested_list_complex_prop:         <- list_complex_prop index 0
      - a                               <- list_complex_prop index 0, nested_list_complex_prop index 0
      - b                               <- list_complex_prop index 0, nested_list_complex_prop index 1
      - c                               <- list_complex_prop index 0, nested_list_complex_prop index 2
      - d                               <- list_complex_prop index 0, nested_list_complex_prop index 3
    - nested_list_complex_prop:         <- list_complex_prop index 1
      - e                               <- list_complex_prop index 1, nested_list_complex_prop index 0
      - f                               <- list_complex_prop index 1, nested_list_complex_prop index 1
      - g                               <- list_complex_prop index 1, nested_list_complex_prop index 2
      - h                               <- list_complex_prop index 1, nested_list_complex_prop index 3

then "{ get_property: [ my_prop2, list_complex_prop, 1, nested_list_complex_prop, 3]" would resolve to "h"

Patch Set 7:

When it is possible to use an index it should be mandatory, for example, if setting a value for an integer property and we allow the user to select a list of integers, then an index must be provided. It is not valid otherwise, as would be setting a list value to an integer

When I set a numeric index, export and import I am getting an error. ClassCastException is being thrown from ToscaFunctionYamlParsingHandler line 55

It should also be possible to select nested lists, e.g. a list nested in a complex type. I dont think that is possible with this change, but Ive no problem with that being taken as a separate story if want to get this merged

Patch Set 8:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> When it is possible to use an index it should be mandatory, for example, if setting a value for an integer property and we allow the user to select a list of integers, then an index must be provided. It is not valid otherwise, as would be setting a list value to an integer
> 
> When I set a numeric index, export and import I am getting an error. ClassCastException is being thrown from ToscaFunctionYamlParsingHandler line 55
> 
> It should also be possible to select nested lists, e.g. a list nested in a complex type. I dont think that is possible with this change, but Ive no problem with that being taken as a separate story if want to get this merged

Exception is resolved and made index mandatory when it is visible.

We can make a separate story for the nested list for complex type.
